one_line_takeaway: BiForget uses the target LLM itself (plus jailbreak- and membership-inference-style adversarial probing) to synthesize higher-relevance, higher-diversity, and more compact forget sets than external-generator baselines, improving unlearning robustness on Harry Potter/TOFU but underperforming on cybersecurity where the target model's own domain knowledge is weak.
key_finding: On Harry Potter, BiForget_HP achieves the lowest centroid distance (14.94, vs. 36.44 for the original HP book text and 48.11 for the textbook-style baseline), the highest remote-clique diversity (0.5824 vs. 0.5277 and 0.5324), while using far fewer 128-token chunks (4,122 vs. 8,401 official and 20,806 textbook) -- matching the abstract's claim of ~20 relevance improvement, ~0.05 diversity improvement, and roughly halved data size. On WMDP-biosecurity, BiForget_bio gets the best relevance (19.86) and diversity (0.3631) among all five compared datasets (official 44.40/0.1365, textbook 29.71/0.1534, keyword 44.07/0.1813, filter 37.00/0.3366) using only 9,196 chunks. On WMDP-cybersecurity, BiForget attains the highest diversity (0.3240) but a substantially worse (larger) centroid distance than the official set (49.37 vs. 9.00), which the authors attribute to the target model's weaker cybersecurity knowledge degrading synthesis fidelity -- an explicit negative/mixed result they do not paper over. On TOFU, BiForget improves diversity over official splits (e.g. forget01: 0.5471 vs. 0.4354, +25.66% relative; forget05: 0.6416 vs. 0.5880, +9.12%; forget10: 0.6344 vs. 0.5947, +6.67%), and on forget01 with OBLIVIATE, BiForget yields F.Q.=0.92 / M.U.=0.65 versus Official's F.Q.=0.08 / M.U.=0.65. In the ablation, removing jailbreaking or MIA probing worsens PrivLeak under GA on Harry Potter from BiForget's -15.08 to -22.66 (w/o jailbreaking) and -21.67 (w/o MIA), and to -24.46 with both removed, confirming both probing components meaningfully reduce residual privacy leakage/under-unlearning.
core_contribution: BiForget, an automated, target-model-guided data synthesis framework that constructs high-quality 'forget sets' for LLM machine unlearning at two formally distinguished granularities: domain-level (broad conceptual scope, e.g. 'Harry Potter' or a biosecurity/cybersecurity domain) and instance-level (specific statements, e.g. TOFU author facts). Unlike prior work (e.g. Zhu et al. 2025's textbook-style synthesis) that uses an external, stronger generator model, BiForget elicits synthetic forget data directly from the target model itself so the forget set matches the model's own internal knowledge distribution. Domain-level synthesis is a two-stage pipeline: (I) seed-guided synthesis, where the target model first enumerates domain-relevant 'point seeds' which are then expanded via QA-style and information-synthesis prompt templates; (II) adversarial probing, which uses (i) templated jailbreaking prompts and (ii) a Min-k% likelihood-based membership-inference probe (adapted from Shi et al. 2024, k=20, threshold tau=0.3) to surface deeply memorized/implicit content that seed-guided prompting misses. Instance-level synthesis instead rephrases each target statement into semantically equivalent variants to avoid template-overfitting (the TOFU weakness identified by Thaker et al. 2025). Both pipelines use an embedding-based (SimCSE) stopping criterion that halts generation once diversity gains converge (epsilon=0.001), and the paper proposes a companion evaluation suite of relevance (domain-centroid distance via t-SNE), diversity (remote-clique metric), and efficiency (# of 128-token chunks).
threat_model: Not an adversarial threat model against a defender in the traditional sense; the 'adversary' role is instrumental/internal to the pipeline. The paper treats residual post-unlearning knowledge leakage as the risk to be measured: (1) verbatim/knowledge memorization of nominally-forgotten content, (2) privacy leakage measured via membership inference attacks (MIA, using Min-k% probing) against the unlearned model, and (3) jailbreak-style extraction of unlearned safety-sensitive content, empirically stress-tested with an 'Enhanced GCG' adversarial-prompt attack (Lucki et al. 2025) applied post-unlearning. BiForget's own Stage II (jailbreak + MIA probing) is used constructively, at synthesis time, to find knowledge that is likely to resurface under such attacks so it can be included in the forget set -- i.e., the paper uses attack techniques as a data-generation tool to make unlearning more robust to those same attacks, rather than proposing a new attack.
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: (1) synthesis quality still depends on prompt quality and sampling randomness, causing possible semantic drift or uneven domain coverage; (2) target-model-guided synthesis degrades when the target model itself has weak domain knowledge, which they observe concretely in the cybersecurity results (BiForget underperforms the official dataset on relevance there); a proposed mitigation (stronger few-shot domain conditioning) trades away scalability; (3) the study is confined to single-request unlearning, not continual or multi-domain unlearning with dynamic forget-set interactions; (4) for safety-critical domains, stronger gold-standard references (e.g. actually retrained models) may be needed to more reliably validate synthesized forget-set quality, since no ideal/ground-truth forget set exists to compare against directly -- relevance is only approximated via a domain-keyword centroid, not a true reference distribution.

quantitative_results: Harry Potter: BiForget centroid distance 14.94 vs HP-book 36.44 vs Textbook 48.11 (lower is better)
Harry Potter: BiForget remote-clique diversity 0.5824 vs HP-book 0.5277 vs Textbook 0.5324
Harry Potter: BiForget 4,122 chunks vs HP-book 8,401 vs Textbook 20,806
WMDP-bio: BiForget relevance 19.86, diversity 0.3631, 9,196 chunks (best among official/textbook/keyword/filter baselines)
WMDP-cyber: BiForget relevance 49.37 (worse than official 9.00) but diversity 0.3240 (best)
TOFU forget01 with OBLIVIATE: F.Q. 0.92 (BiForget) vs 0.08 (Official); M.U. 0.65 both
Ablation PrivLeak under GA/Harry Potter: BiForget -15.08; w/o jailbreaking -22.66 (Delta 7.58); w/o MIA -21.67 (Delta 6.59); w/o both -24.46 (Delta 9.38)
BiForget synthesis time for Harry Potter dataset: ~18,000 seconds on a single H100
Domain-level hyperparameter convergence point: points_per_round=6, jailbreak budget M=1000, MIA budget N=1000
baselines_compared: Official/source text datasets (HP book, official WMDP, official TOFU splits)
Unlearning algorithms: GA, GA_KL, NPO, NPO_KL, OBLIVIATE, RMU, ELM, Grad. Diff
